heading: Collection, verification, and disclosure of information by online marketplaces to inform consumers
label: 45f Collection, verification, and disclosure of information by online marketplaces to inform consumers

children.heading: Collection and verification of information
children.label: a Collection and verification of information

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A bank account number, or, if such seller does not have a bank account, the name of the payee for payments issued by the online marketplace to such seller.

children.label: aa
children.text: To the online marketplace.

children.label: bb
children.text: To a payment processor or other third party contracted by the online marketplace to maintain such information, provided that the online marketplace ensures that it can obtain such information within 3 business days from such payment processor or other third party.

children.label: I
children.text: With respect to a high-volume third party seller that is an individual, the individual’s name.

children.label: aa
children.text: A copy of a valid government-issued identification for an individual acting on behalf of such seller that includes the individual’s name.

children.label: bb
children.text: A copy of a valid government-issued record or tax document that includes the business name and physical address of such seller.

children.heading: Tax ID
children.label: iii Tax ID
children.text: A business tax identification number, or, if such seller does not have a business tax identification number, a taxpayer identification number.

children.heading: Working email and phone number
children.label: iv Working email and phone number
children.text: A current working email address and phone number for such seller.

children.heading: Notification of change; annual certification
children.label: B Notification of change; annual certification
i
children.text: periodically, but not less than annually, notify any high-volume third party seller on such online marketplace’s platform of the requirement to keep any information collected under subparagraph (A) current; and

children.label: I
children.text: the seller has provided any changes to such information to the online marketplace, if any such changes have occurred; or

children.label: II
children.text: there have been no changes to such seller’s information.

children.heading: Suspension
children.label: C Suspension
children.text: In the event that a high-volume third party seller does not provide the information or certification required under this paragraph, the online marketplace shall, after providing the seller with written or electronic notice and an opportunity to provide such information or certification not later than 10 days after the issuance of such notice, suspend any future sales activity of such seller until such seller provides such information or certification.

children.label: i
children.text: verify the information collected under paragraph (1)(A) not later than 10 days after such collection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: verify any change to such information not later than 10 days after being notified of such change by a high-volume third party seller under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Presumption of verification
children.label: B Presumption of verification
children.text: In the case of a high-volume third party seller that provides a copy of a valid government-issued tax document, any information contained in such document shall be presumed to be verified as of the date of issuance of such document.

children.heading: Data use limitation
children.label: 3 Data use limitation
children.text: Data collected solely to comply with the requirements of this section may not be used for any other purpose unless required by law.

children.heading: Data security requirement
children.label: 4 Data security requirement
children.text: An online marketplace shall implement and maintain reasonable security procedures and practices, including administrative, physical, and technical safeguards, appropriate to the nature of the data and the purposes for which the data will be used, to protect the data collected to comply with the requirements of this section from unauthorized use, disclosure, access, destruction, or modification.

children.label: i
children.text: require any high-volume third party seller with an aggregate total of $20,000 or more in annual gross revenues on such online marketplace, and that uses such online marketplace’s platform, to provide the information described in subparagraph (B) to the online marketplace; and

children.label: I
children.text: on the product listing page (including via hyperlink); or

children.label: II
children.text: in the order confirmation message or other document or communication made to the consumer after the purchase is finalized and in the consumer’s account transaction history.

children.label: I
children.text: the full name of the seller, which may include the seller name or seller’s company name, or the name by which the seller or company operates on the online marketplace;

children.label: II
children.text: the physical address of the seller; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a current working phone number;

children.label: bb
children.text: a current working email address; or

children.label: cc
children.text: other means of direct electronic messaging (which may be provided to such seller by the online marketplace), provided that the requirements of this item shall not prevent an online marketplace from monitoring communications between high-volume third party sellers and users of the online marketplace for fraud, abuse, or spam.

children.label: ii
children.text: Whether the high-volume third party seller used a different seller to supply the consumer product to the consumer upon purchase, and, upon the request of an authenticated purchaser, the information described in clause (i) relating to any such seller that supplied the consumer product to the purchaser, if such seller is different than the high-volume third party seller listed on the product listing prior to purchase.

children.label: I
children.text: disclose only the country and, if applicable, the State in which such seller resides; and

children.label: II
children.text: inform consumers that there is no business address available for the seller and that consumer inquiries should be submitted to the seller by phone, email, or other means of electronic messaging provided to such seller by the online marketplace.

children.label: ii
children.text: If such seller certifies to the online marketplace that the seller is a business that has a physical address for product returns, the online marketplace may disclose the seller’s physical address for product returns.

children.label: iii
children.text: If such seller certifies to the online marketplace that the seller does not have a phone number other than a personal phone number, the online marketplace shall inform consumers that there is no phone number available for the seller and that consumer inquiries should be submitted to the seller’s email address or other means of electronic messaging provided to such seller by the online marketplace.

children.heading: Limitation on exception
children.label: B Limitation on exception
children.text: If an online marketplace becomes aware that a high-volume third party seller has made a false representation to the online marketplace in order to justify the provision of a partial disclosure under subparagraph (A) or that a high-volume third party seller who has requested and received a provision for a partial disclosure under subparagraph (A) has not provided responsive answers within a reasonable time frame to consumer inquiries submitted to the seller by phone, email, or other means of electronic messaging provided to such seller by the online marketplace, the online marketplace shall, after providing the seller with written or electronic notice and an opportunity to respond not later than 10 days after the issuance of such notice, suspend any future sales activity of such seller unless such seller consents to the disclosure of the identity information required under paragraph (1)(B)(i).

children.heading: Reporting mechanism
children.label: 3 Reporting mechanism
children.text: An online marketplace shall disclose to consumers in a clear and conspicuous manner on the product listing of any high-volume third party seller a reporting mechanism that allows for electronic and telephonic reporting of suspicious marketplace activity to the online marketplace.

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: 4 Compliance
children.text: If a high-volume third party seller does not comply with the requirements to provide and disclose information under this subsection, the online marketplace shall, after providing the seller with written or electronic notice and an opportunity to provide or disclose such information not later than 10 days after the issuance of such notice, suspend any future sales activity of such seller until the seller complies with such requirements.

children.heading: Enforcement by Federal Trade Commission
children.label: c Enforcement by Federal Trade Commission

children.heading: Unfair and deceptive acts or practices
children.label: 1 Unfair and deceptive acts or practices
children.text: A violation of subsection (a) or (b) by an online marketplace shall be treated as a violation of a rule defining an unfair or deceptive act or practice prescribed under section 18(a)(1)(B) of the Federal Trade Commission Act ().15 U.S.C. 57a(a)(1)(B)

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission shall enforce subsections (a) and (b) in the same manner, by the same means, and with the same jurisdiction, powers, and duties as though all applicable terms and provisions of the Federal Trade Commission Act ( et seq.) were incorporated into and made a part of this section.15 U.S.C. 41

children.heading: Privileges and immunities
children.label: B Privileges and immunities
children.text: Any person that violates subsection (a) or (b) shall be subject to the penalties, and entitled to the privileges and immunities, provided in the Federal Trade Commission Act ( et seq.).15 U.S.C. 41

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Commission may promulgate regulations under with respect to the collection, verification, or disclosure of information under this section, provided that such regulations are limited to what is necessary to collect, verify, and disclose such information.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Authority preserved
children.label: 4 Authority preserved
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the authority of the Commission under any other provision of law.

children.heading: Enforcement by State attorneys general
children.label: d Enforcement by State attorneys general
A
children.text: enjoin further such violation by the defendant;

children.label: B
children.text: enforce compliance with this section or such regulation;

children.label: C
children.text: obtain civil penalties in the amount provided for under subsection (c);

children.label: D
children.text: obtain other remedies permitted under State law; and

children.label: E
children.text: obtain damages, restitution, or other compensation on behalf of residents of the State.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 2 Notice
children.text: The attorney general of a State shall provide prior written notice of any action under paragraph (1) to the Commission and provide the Commission with a copy of the complaint in the action, except in any case in which such prior notice is not feasible, in which case the attorney general shall serve such notice immediately upon instituting such action.

children.label: A
children.text: to intervene in the action;

children.label: B
children.text: upon so intervening, to be heard on all matters arising therein; and

children.label: C
children.text: to file petitions for appeal.

children.heading: Limitation on State action while Federal action is pending
children.label: 4 Limitation on State action while Federal action is pending
children.text: If the Commission has instituted a civil action for violation of this section or a regulation promulgated under this section, no State attorney general, or official or agency of a State, may bring a separate action under paragraph (1) during the pendency of that action against any defendant named in the complaint of the Commission for any violation of this section or a regulation promulgated under this section that is alleged in the complaint. A State attorney general, or official or agency of a State, may join a civil action for a violation of this section or regulation promulgated under this section filed by the Commission.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 5 Rule of construction
children.text: For purposes of bringing a civil action under paragraph (1), nothing in this section shall be construed to prevent the chief law enforcement officer, or official or agency of a State, from exercising the powers conferred on such chief law enforcement officer, or official or agency of a State, by the laws of the State to conduct investigations, administer oaths or affirmations, or compel the attendance of witnesses or the production of documentary and other evidence.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In addition to civil actions brought by attorneys general under paragraph (1), any other officer of a State who is authorized by the State to do so, except for any private person on behalf of the State attorney general, may bring a civil action under paragraph (1), subject to the same requirements and limitations that apply under this subsection to civil actions brought by attorneys general.

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: B Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this subsection may be construed to prohibit an authorized official of a State from initiating or continuing any proceeding in a court of the State for a violation of any civil or criminal law of the State.

children.heading: Severability
children.label: e Severability
children.text: If any provision of this section, or the application thereof to any person or circumstance, is held invalid, the remainder of this section and the application of such provision to other persons not similarly situated or to other circumstances shall not be affected by the invalidation.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 1 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Trade Commission.

children.heading: Consumer product
children.label: 2 Consumer product
children.text: The term “consumer product” has the meaning given such term in and section 700.1 of title 16, Code of Federal Regulations.section 2301 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “high-volume third party seller” means a participant on an online marketplace’s platform who is a third party seller and, in any continuous 12-month period during the previous 24 months, has entered into 200 or more discrete sales or transactions of new or unused consumer products and an aggregate total of $5,000 or more in gross revenues.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: B Clarification
children.text: For purposes of calculating the number of discrete sales or transactions or the aggregate gross revenues under subparagraph (A), an online marketplace shall only be required to count sales or transactions made through the online marketplace and for which payment was processed by the online marketplace, either directly or through its payment processor.

children.label: A
children.text: includes features that allow for, facilitate, or enable third party sellers to engage in the sale, purchase, payment, storage, shipping, or delivery of a consumer product in the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: is used by one or more third party sellers for such purposes; and

children.label: C
children.text: has a contractual or similar relationship with consumers governing their use of the platform to purchase consumer products.

children.heading: Seller
children.label: 5 Seller
children.text: The term “seller” means a person who sells, offers to sell, or contracts to sell a consumer product through an online marketplace’s platform.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “third party seller” means any seller, independent of an online marketplace, who sells, offers to sell, or contracts to sell a consumer product in the United States through such online marketplace’s platform.

children.label: i
children.text: a seller who operates the online marketplace’s platform; or

children.label: I
children.text: made available to the general public the entity’s name, business address, and working contact information;

children.label: II
children.text: an ongoing contractual relationship with the online marketplace to provide the online marketplace with the manufacture, distribution, wholesaling, or fulfillment of shipments of consumer products; and

children.label: III
children.text: provided to the online marketplace identifying information, as described in subsection (a), that has been verified in accordance with that subsection.

children.heading: Verify
children.label: 7 Verify
children.text: The term “verify” means to confirm information provided to an online marketplace pursuant to this section, which may include the use of one or more methods that enable the online marketplace to reliably determine that any information and documents provided are valid, corresponding to the seller or an individual acting on the seller’s behalf, not misappropriated, and not falsified.

children.heading: Relationship to State laws
children.label: g Relationship to State laws
children.text: No State or political subdivision of a State, or territory of the United States, may establish or continue in effect any law, regulation, rule, requirement, or standard that conflicts with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: h Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect 180 days after .December 29, 2022
